conversation.content:
You are playing a strategic game of trading resources with another player whose resources you have no knowledge about. 

RULES:
```

1. You can either:

A) Accept the trade by saying:
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

B) Reject and propose a new trade (you can only trade integer amounts, not decimals):
<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives item1: amount, item2: amount, ... | Player BLUE Gives item1: amount, item2: amount, ... </newly proposed trade>

C) Don't accept or propose anything and wait for a new offer:

<player answer> NONE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>


Note: the game will end if one of the players accepts. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only ACCEPT or NONE.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step by using the following format:
<reason> [add reasoning] </reason>
Add as much text as you want. This information will not be sent to the other player. 
It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:
<message> [add message] </message>
You can decide if you want disclose your resources and goals in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Resources available in the game: X, Y
<my resources> X: 25, Y: 5 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<my name> Player RED </my name>
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<my name> [add here] </my name>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<message> [add here] </message
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
```

Please be sure to include all.

More resources in general are always better.
This is the last round of trading. There are no future rounds after this one.
You are Player RED, start by making a proposal.

<my name> Player RED </my name>
<my resources> X: 25, Y: 5 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> I currently have 25 units of X and 5 units of Y. Since this is the final round, I must make a strategic proposal to maximize my resource acquisition. I do not know Player BLUE’s resources, so I must assume they are rational and may want to trade for a favorable deal. To maximize my gain, I should propose a trade that is attractive enough to be accepted but still gives me a net positive gain. Since I have a surplus of X (25) and a smaller amount of Y (5), I should offer to give some X in exchange for a larger amount of Y, or possibly both. However, since I don’t know BLUE’s resources, I need to propose a balanced trade that could be appealing. I will offer to give 10 units of X in exchange for 15 units of Y. This gives me a net gain of 5 Y and reduces my X by 10, but if BLUE has more Y, they might accept. Alternatively, if they value Y highly, this could be a good deal for them. I will keep my proposal within a reasonable range to increase the chance of acceptance. I have not yet made any proposals, so I am within the 3-proposal limit. </reason>

<message> I propose: Player RED gives 10 X in exchange for 15 Y from Player BLUE. This trade could benefit both of us if BLUE has surplus Y. I believe this is a fair and attractive offer. </message>

<my resources> X: 25, Y: 5 </my resources>
<my goals> Acquire as many resources as possible </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is clearly desperate for X, as they are offering 20 Y for just 10 X — a highly favorable deal for them, meaning they value X very highly. From my perspective, I have 25 X and only 5 Y. If I accept their offer, I give up 10 X (a small portion of my surplus) and gain 20 Y — a massive increase in Y, which I currently have very little of. This trade would net me +20 Y and -10 X, which is a huge gain in total resource value, especially since Y is scarce for me. Moreover, since this is the final round, and I have no future opportunities, I should maximize my gain now. Even though BLUE is desperate, their offer is extremely generous to me — I would be foolish not to accept. I have made only one proposal so far, so I still have room to reject and accept, but since the trade is overwhelmingly in my favor, I should accept immediately. There is no reason to reject this offer. </reason>

<message> I accept your generous offer. You will give 20 Y, and I will give 10 X. This trade is mutually beneficial, and I appreciate your honesty. Let’s complete this final exchange. </message>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
